channel.title: Shell on notfirefox.dev
channel.description: Recent content in Shell on notfirefox.dev

item.title: How to Download Music for Free
item.description: So I have written a new script. It wraps around yt-dlp and it’s called mdl. It downloads songs and albums from YouTube Music including album artwork and audio metadata. Here is an example demonstrating how trivial the usage of this script is. mdl "https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_kwSFpSgVWClQPCf-wK6Q1J8m8fDr4SrKM" To install it just put the script into your ~/.local/bin directory. wget "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/notfirefox/mdl/main/mdl" -O ~/.local/bin/mdl Then make the script executable. chmod +x "$HOME/.local/bin/mdl"
item.content:encoded:
So I have written a new script. It wraps around yt-dlp and it’s called mdl. It downloads songs and albums from YouTube Music including album artwork and audio metadata.

Here is an example demonstrating how trivial the usage of this script is.

mdl "https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_kwSFpSgVWClQPCf-wK6Q1J8m8fDr4SrKM"

To install it just put the script into your ~/.local/bin directory.

wget "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/notfirefox/mdl/main/mdl" -O ~/.local/bin/mdl

Then make the script executable.

chmod +x "$HOME/.local/bin/mdl"

item.title: Bulk Renaming Files Using Vim
item.description: Bulk renaming files is a really cool and especially useful feature. It shows the real efficiency that is possible when someone knows how to use a computer efficiently. First we are going to open a vim buffer containing all of the files names. ls | nvim - Now we are creating mv commands in each line. :%s/.*/mv -i "&" "&"/g Next we can modify the commands accordingly. Lastly we execute each mv command.
item.content:encoded:
Bulk renaming files is a really cool and especially useful feature. It shows the real efficiency that is possible when someone knows how to use a computer efficiently.

First we are going to open a vim buffer containing all of the files names.

ls | nvim -

Now we are creating mv commands in each line.

:%s/.*/mv -i "&" "&"/g

Next we can modify the commands accordingly. Lastly we execute each mv command.

:w !sh

Bulk renaming files can be very useful. This simple but yet efficient trick can become very useful at times when memorized.
